core_identity.title: Cumulative Frequency: Who Is Most Affected?
core_identity.author: Gerry Docherty

curriculum_alignment.subject: mathematics
curriculum_alignment.subtopic: cumulative frequency tables and graphs (estimating median and quartiles)
curriculum_alignment.seeds_of_change: Construct and interpret cumulative frequency tables and graphs; use them to estimate and interpret medians and quartiles in real-world (SDG) contexts.

verse_ality.driving_question: How can cumulative frequency help us see who is most affected?

knowing_world.focus: Using cumulative frequency to show how data accumulates and to estimate median and quartiles (Q1, Q3) from tables and cumulative frequency graphs (ogives), using SDG-themed data.
key_concepts: Cumulative frequency table from a frequency table
Cumulative frequency graph (ogive): plotting (upper class boundary, cumulative frequency)
tools: Interactive: Cumulative Frequency Table Builder (SDG Data) (canva_embed): https://www.canva.com/design/DAG_Cw4F2PY/tSSOM6iJAfCeCa0jWxqzGg/view?embed
Interactive: Cumulative Frequency Graph Explorer (SDG Contexts) (canva_embed): https://www.canva.com/design/DAG_DMG94f0/tqBkcZQgZVpXEI0X2YoUuA/view?embed

human_story.focus: Cumulative frequency helps us see how many people are at or below each value, which can reveal inequality and who sits in the 'tail' of a distribution in SDG contexts like sleep, water use, energy use, and commute times.
justice_prompts: Who might be most affected by being in the tail of the distribution (e.g., longest commutes, lowest sleep, highest water use), and how might that impact health, learning or wellbeing?

expressive_self.focus: Treat the cumulative frequency curve as a 'journey' where more people are included as you move along the x-axis, and communicate meaning using sketches, labels, and personification.
activities: At the start, almost nobody is included.
As we move along the x-axis (time, hours, usage), more and more people join the total.
Eventually, everyone is included at the top of the curve (total frequency N).
Draw a sketch of the cumulative frequency curve and label where the median and quartiles are. Add speech bubbles like: “Half of us are here by this point” or “Most of us are included by here.”
Write a short paragraph or poem that personifies the curve (e.g. “I climb steadily as more teenagers get enough sleep…”).
💬 If you think of yourself as a point on the curve in one context (e.g. sleep time), where would you like to be, and why?

designed_world.focus: Use a comparator to place two cumulative frequency curves on the same axes, estimate medians and quartiles for each group, and write evidence-based comparison statements about typical experience and variation in SDG contexts.
design_challenges: Interactive: Cumulative Frequency Graph Comparator (SDG Stories) (canva_embed): https://www.canva.com/design/DAG_DNaIojg/hTDNH3DV8SgEMVmKR0CEUQ/view?embed
Select an SDG scenario comparing two groups (e.g., water use, commute times, study hours).
Estimate each group’s median from the curves at cumulative frequency N/2.
Estimate Q1 and Q3 for each group at N/4 and 3N/4, then compute IQR (Q3 − Q1).
Compare: which group has the higher median and which has the larger IQR (more variation).
Compare early parts of the curves: for ‘better’ lower values (e.g., shorter commutes, more sleep), which group reaches higher cumulative frequency sooner?
Buddy challenge: write a joint comparison statement using median and quartiles as evidence.
Ethical design: what extra context should decision-makers see so they don’t oversimplify or blame a group based only on one graph?

regulation_supports: Look away from the screen and name 3 things you can see in the room.
Take a slow breath in for 4 counts, and out for 6 counts.
Gently stretch your shoulders and hands, then return to the graph.
wellbeing_prompts: How can you use data about your life (sleep, movement, travel) to support your wellbeing, rather than judge yourself?

pathways.name: Option A – One Group, Deep Dive
pathways.focus: Use one SDG context to build/interpret a cumulative frequency table or graph and explain what the median and IQR say about the group’s experience.
products: Screenshot(s) or copied values from the cumulative frequency table/graph used.
Positions for Q1, median and Q3 (N/4, N/2, 3N/4).
Estimated values of Q1, median and Q3 from the table/graph, plus IQR (Q3 − Q1).
A 6–8 sentence paragraph explaining what the median and IQR show in the chosen SDG context.
criteria: Conceptual Understanding: correct use of cumulative frequency, median and quartiles to describe a distribution.
Fluency & Accuracy: positions (N/4, N/2, 3N/4) and readings from the graph/table are clear and sensible.
Application to Problems: interprets results in the SDG context (not just abstract numbers).
Independence & Reflection: explains in own words who is most affected and what the data suggests for action or change.

pathways.name: Option B – Two Groups Compared (SDG Story)
pathways.focus: Compare two groups using cumulative frequency curves, estimating median and IQR for each and writing an evidence-based comparison about ‘better’ outcomes and variation.
products: Screenshot(s) or copied values from the cumulative frequency comparison graph used.
Estimated median for Group A and Group B.
Estimated Q1 and Q3 for each group and each IQR.
A comparison paragraph stating which group generally has the better outcome in context (e.g., shorter commutes/more sleep) and which shows more variation, with evidence from medians and quartiles.
criteria: Conceptual Understanding: compares distributions using median and quartiles appropriately.
Fluency & Accuracy: estimates are consistent with the curves and calculations are clear.
Application to Problems: connects comparisons to what the SDG context means for people.
Independence & Reflection: avoids blame; suggests what extra context would support fair interpretation.

required: Add running totals to build a cumulative frequency column
Understand median and quartiles as positions in ordered data (N/2, N/4, 3N/4)
Read values from a graph using horizontal/vertical tracing

builds_toward: Cumulative frequency graphs to estimate median and quartiles in exam questions
Box plots and comparing distributions using median and IQR
Data interpretation and fairness when comparing groups
